response: The Latin name for one of the five "big cats" that the word "Panther" may refer to when outside of the Americas is **Panthera pardus**. This is the scientific name for the leopard, which is one of the five big cats in the genus *Panthera*. The leopard is part of the family Felidae and is commonly referred to as a "leopard" in English, and "Panther" is used in various regions to denote this species.
